title: solanum melongena subsp. insanum
intro: Solanum melongena subsp. insanum, also known as the bitter eggplant or devil's eggplant, is a subspecies of the eggplant plant, native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its bitter and toxic properties. The plant contains solanine, a glycoalkaloid that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Solanum melongena subsp. insanum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, although its toxicity means it should be used with caution. Herbal preparations of the plant include tinctures and infusions, which are typically used in small amounts under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
